subject: Economics
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the three dimensions of food security: Availability, Accessibility, and Affordability
Identify why food security is essential and who is food insecure
Learn about the role of the Government in ensuring food security through PDS and Buffer Stock
Understand the concepts of Minimum Support Price (MSP) and Issue Price
Recognize the role of cooperatives in food security

lesson_plan.title: What is Food Security?
topics_covered: Three dimensions: Availability, Accessibility, and Affordability
Impact of natural calamities (e.g., Famine of Bengal)
teaching_strategy: Discussion using real-life examples of price rises
student_activities: List the three pillars of food security in notebooks
Discuss what happens to food supply during a drought
assessment: Oral questioning on the three dimensions

lesson_plan.title: Who are Food Insecure?
topics_covered: Vulnerable groups in rural and urban areas
teaching_strategy: Comparison table of urban vs. rural food insecure people
Brainstorming on the causes of chronic hunger
student_activities: Identify types of hunger in provided short stories
Draw a table comparing seasonal and chronic hunger

lesson_plan.title: India’s Path to Self-Sufficiency
topics_covered: Indira Gandhi and the 'Wheat Revolution' stamp
teaching_strategy: Analysis of production graphs from the textbook
Discussion on why India hasn't faced a famine since the 1970s
student_activities: Interpret a bar graph of food grain production
List states that benefited most from the Green Revolution

lesson_plan.title: Food Security System in India: Buffer Stock
topics_covered: Role of Food Corporation of India (FCI)
teaching_strategy: Explanation of why the government stores grain
student_activities: Draw a flowchart showing the movement of grain from farm to FCI
Define MSP in their own words

lesson_plan.title: Public Distribution System (PDS)
topics_covered: Ration shops and Fair Price Shops
Types of Ration Cards (Antyodaya, BPL, APL)
teaching_strategy: Discussion on the items available at a local ration shop
student_activities: Visit a local ration shop or interview someone who uses one
List items found in a typical Fair Price Shop

lesson_plan.title: Current Status of PDS & Government Schemes
topics_covered: Targeted PDS (TPDS) and Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY)
teaching_strategy: Short lecture on the evolution of PDS
student_activities: Identify the benefits of the Mid-day Meal scheme
Match schemes with their target groups

lesson_plan.title: Role of Cooperatives & Revision
topics_covered: Success stories: Amul and Mother Dairy
Grain banks set up by NGOs
teaching_strategy: Mind mapping the whole chapter on the board
Discussion on how milk and vegetables are distributed in cities
student_activities: Participate in a chapter-end group quiz
Complete the summary mind map in notebooks

teaching_aids: Images of FCI godowns and grain storage
teacher_tip: Use the local 'Fair Price Shop' as a concrete example to help students understand the abstract concept of 'Affordability' and 'Accessibility'.
